payload.text:
on mobile, the title is not showing on the volunteer form.. make it show

remove the white BG on the hero section image on the homepage

**Examining Display Issues**

I'm currently looking into the mobile display issue with the volunteer form. I've narrowed it down to potential issues in `features/about-us/AboutPage.tsx` and `components/VolunteerOverlay.tsx`. My goal is to confirm headings are consistently visible across all screen sizes. I also removed the home page hero image as requested.


**Removing Unnecessary Styling**

I've been looking at the white background issue on the homepage hero image. I've pinpointed the styling culprit: excessive shadow and border properties in `HomePage.tsx`. I'm removing the `shadow-xl` and `border border-gray-100` classes to resolve this.


**Analyzing Mobile Visibility**

I'm now focusing on mobile display. I noticed the volunteer form is wrapped in a blue container with padding that should show content. Investigating further, the `Reveal` component or a parent element might be clipping the form's content. Alternatively, the "title not showing" might refer to the `h2` outside the form, which *is* present in the code. I'm focusing on why the title isn't consistently visible.


**Addressing Title Visibility**

I'm now zeroing in on the title visibility issue on mobile. While the `p-6` padding is fine, I'm checking if `pt-[140px]` or something similar is causing overlap elsewhere. The user specifically flagged the title in `VolunteerOverlay.tsx`, where I see the title is "Volunteer with Us." I'm reviewing how this title renders in the `VolunteerOverlay` component, and also re-evaluating the positioning and display of the heading in `AboutPage.tsx` since that component includes the volunteer form on mobile.


**Clarifying Title and Image**

I'm now clarifying the title visibility issue and hero image background. While I understand the "Volunteer with us" title is inside the blue card, I'll confirm the `Reveal` or its container isn't obscuring it. Also, I'm now looking at why they wanted the white background "removed". The hero image itself, `PHA_Hero_Imag_te6vxv.png`, might have a white background, which I can't directly change. If they mean the section's background or the box around the image, I'll examine the CSS for modifications.


**Adjusting Background and Styling**

I'm now focusing on background modifications and hero image styling. I initially thought the white background issue was about the image itself. However, it seems the client wants to change the section's background. I'll test swapping `bg-[#f8fafc]` to `bg-white` in `HomePage.tsx`. Regarding the hero image, I'll remove `border border-gray-100 shadow-xl`. I'll also check again that the `h2` and `h3` titles in the `VolunteerOverlay` are clearly visible on mobile within their blue container, even with the `reveal` wrapper.


**Optimizing Image and Titles**

I'm now focusing on optimizing the hero image and volunteer form titles. The recent feedback suggests I need to ensure the titles are clear on mobile within the `VolunteerOverlay.tsx`. I'll revisit `z-index` and `sticky` properties, as well as the rendering of `h2` elements. I also updated the hero image class to `object-contain` for clearer display.


**Confirming Title Visibility**

I'm now going to focus on the volunteer form title visibility. I will update `AboutPage.tsx` to ensure the "Volunteer with us" heading is clear on mobile. I'm prioritizing the title's display by examining the hero image styling and the "Volunteer" form as well. My plan involves cleaning up the hero image styling and confirming title visibility. I will ensure this title is visible and consider removing or adjusting the `bg-white/95` background.


**Updating Backgrounds and Titles**

I've tested setting the section background in `HomePage.tsx` to `bg-white`, and removed the border and shadow from the hero image. Now, I am refining the title visibility on mobile for the volunteer form, specifically in `AboutPage.tsx` and `VolunteerOverlay.tsx`. I'll confirm that the titles, especially the "Volunteer with us" heading, remain clear against the white background, and consider further styling if needed.

payload.text:
on the Partner and sponsor form, make the check boxes to have no background just outline

Remove volunteer, mentor/facilitator from the selection from the options and add a become a mentor button on the bootcamp page after the list of mentor section.

make the expectations to be a drop down selection field

Reduce the button text size on mobile to be 14px

**Updating Form Elements**

I've been focusing on the feedback regarding the form design. Specifically, I'm working on removing the background fill from the checkboxes and removing "Volunteer" and "Mentor/Facilitator" from the `supporterTypes` list. I've also noted the desire to change the "Expectations" field, but I'm prioritizing the more immediate visual and data structure changes right now.


**Refining Form Interactions**

I'm currently working on transitioning the checkbox grid within the "Expectations" section to a more streamlined `<select>` dropdown. Additionally, I've integrated a "Become a Mentor" call-to-action button after the mentor grid on the bootcamp page. I'm also addressing the visual adjustments, aiming for transparent backgrounds and removing the hover effect for the checkboxes in the `PartnerOverlay`. My immediate focus is on button text size adjustments for mobile.


**Modifying Button Styles**

I'm currently working on adjusting the button text sizes for better mobile responsiveness. I've updated the `Button.tsx` component to use a responsive approach, changing the base text size and then overriding it for larger screens using media queries. I'm also addressing the "no background just outline" feedback for checkboxes. I plan to modify the input style in `PartnerOverlay.tsx`, aiming to have a more streamlined look.


**Revising Checkbox Design**

I'm making progress on the checkbox styling. I've decided to use `appearance-none border border-gray-300 rounded bg-transparent checked:bg-[#135291]` for a custom outlined look to achieve the desired "no background" appearance. I've also pivoted the "Expectations" field to a single-select dropdown for now. It was previously a multi-select checkbox grid, but the term "dropdown selection field" suggests a single selection, and I'll keep it simple by implementing a standard `<select>` element.


**Implementing Mentor CTA**

I've successfully added the "Become a Mentor" call-to-action button to the bootcamp page, positioning it after the mentor list. This required updates to `BootcampsPage.tsx`, leveraging the updated `Button.tsx` component. I've also focused on checkbox styling in `PartnerOverlay.tsx`, removing the background and adjusting the border.  I'm switching the multi-select "Expectations" field to a standard single-select dropdown within `formData`. I've verified that other form overlays don't use checkboxes, so my focus remains on `PartnerOverlay.tsx` and now `formData`.


**Adjusting Supporter Types**

I've been reviewing the supporter types list in `PartnerOverlay.tsx`, removing "Mentor / Facilitator" and "Volunteer" as per the user's feedback. I've also implemented the checkbox styling, ensuring a "no background" appearance with custom border and checked styles. I'm focusing on the input's visual design, aiming for the correct appearance and interaction.


**Enhancing Button Responsiveness**

I've revised the `Button.tsx` component to improve mobile responsiveness. I've adjusted the default text size to 14px and then overridden it with 16px and 18px for medium and large screens, respectively. I've also implemented the checkbox's outlined style with the following:  `after:top-[2px] after:w-[5px] after:h-[10px] after:border-white after:border-b-2 after:border-r-2 after:rotate-45`.  Finally, I've transitioned the "Expectations" field in `PartnerOverlay.tsx` to a standard `<select>` element.

To restore your Google Sheet so that new inputs start at **Line 2**, follow these steps:

1.  Open your Google Sheet.
2.  Select **Row 2** by clicking on the number "2" on the far left.
3.  Scroll to the very bottom of the sheet, hold **Shift**, and click the last row number to select everything from Row 2 downwards.
